items.title: Big Bank CEOs Reckon With Their Lack of Influence on Trump
items.description: Big Bank CEOs Reckon With Their Lack of Influence on Trump

items.title: Bill Ackman’s public campaign to sway Trump on tariffs
items.description: Bill Ackman’s public campaign to slow down the rollout of President Trump’s tariffs continued Tuesday as the billionaire again took to social media to advocate for a pause.

items.title: Wells Fargo: Conservative Provisioning Makes Series DD Preferred Shares A Buy
items.description: Wells Fargo derived 58% of its 2024 revenues from net interest income, higher than large peers but lower than most regional banks. Learn more on WFC stock here.

items.title: Signs of Wall Street stress pile up amid Trump tariff turmoil
items.description: The Trump tariff turmoil triggered new signs of stress on Wall Street, from a freeze in IPOs to a pause in bond sales to a widening of credit spreads.

items.title: What Dimon, Fink, Ackman and Others on Wall Street Are Saying About Tariffs
items.description: Among them are several longtime hedge-fund managers such as Stan Druckenmiller.  “I do not support tariffs exceeding 10%,” he posted on X.  Also on X, Dan Loeb praised an analysis by the American Enterprise Institute that said Trump’s tariff formula “makes no economic sense.”

items.title: Why the Market Dipped But JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) Gained Today
items.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) concluded the recent trading session at $214.05, signifying a +1.79% move from its prior day's close.

items.title: Multiple banks downgraded by Morgan Stanley ahead of earnings
items.description: Financial stocks, like Goldman Sachs (GS), Jefferies (JEF), and Evercore (EVR), are in focus after Morgan Stanley downgraded its outlook for several names in the sector. Yahoo Finance Banking Reporter David Hollerith sits down with Julie Hyman and Trader Talk podcast host Kenny Polcari to check in on the financial sector ahead of earnings. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime here.

items.title: What Chick-fil-A, Chinese tech giants, and a trading scandal taught JP Morgan’s CEO about good leadership
items.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon urged executives to embrace discomfort, stay curious, study competitors, and lead with humility in his latest shareholder letter.

items.title: Four of Jamie Dimon's biggest concerns, per his letter
items.description: JPMorgan Chase's CEO emphasized his concerns about geopolitical conflict and brought up recent culture shifts at the bank in his annual letter to shareholders.
